items.headline: Major commercial project breaks ground on West San Carlos
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial development is underway at 1300 W San Carlos St, marking significant investment in San José's commercial corridor. This is the largest single permit in this batch and represents notable economic activity in the area.

items.headline: City commissions set priorities for coming year
items.summary: Saratoga City Council met with five commissions including Parks, Planning, and Traffic Safety to approve their work plans for fiscal year 2026-27. The meeting gives insight into what city priorities will be addressed over the next year across heritage preservation, public art, recreation, and traffic issues.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city is offering an amnesty program to help businesses at local shopping centers get their licenses in order, along with outreach and incentives for retail and small businesses. This could affect where you shop and which local businesses stay open in Cupertino.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pick-up becomes official city event
items.summary: City Council approved the Great American Litter Pick-up as an annual city-sponsored special event, allowing the city to fund it and accept donated materials and services. Residents can now participate in an officially-backed cleanup effort.

items.headline: City seeks input on federal housing development grants
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission reviewed applications for Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding on March 25, which supports affordable housing and community development projects. These federal funds can significantly impact local housing availability and affordability.

items.headline: San Antonio Road area plan study examines transportation and land use changes
items.summary: The Planning Commission is studying alternatives for how the San Antonio Road area should develop, including transportation improvements and potential land use changes. This could affect traffic patterns and future development in that corridor.

items.headline: Palo Alto amending zoning and building codes to boost retail vitality
items.summary: The Commission is considering updates to zoning and building regulations designed to support the Comprehensive Plan's retail vitality goals. These changes could affect what types of retail businesses can operate where and how storefronts are designed.

items.headline: Campbell updates beekeeping rules with new ordinance
items.summary: The City Council adopted an updated beekeeping ordinance on March 24, which revises regulations for keeping bees within city limits. Residents interested in urban beekeeping should review the new rules to ensure compliance.
